steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The narrator states directly that he “could not remember what [Radha Aunty] looked like” and then immediately goes to the corridor to look at family photographs. This cause-and-effect sequence reveals his purpose.

steps.title: Match the meaning to the choices
steps.content: The narrator is trying to recover a memory—he wants to see what his aunt actually looks like. Choice C matches this exactly: he hopes the photographs will help him recall (bring back to mind) what she looked like. The other choices introduce unsupported elements—writing a story (A), interest in his father's clothing (B), and communicating something to his aunt (D)—that contradict the passage's actual focus on the narrator's personal effort to remember. That matches choice C — C is correct.
